behavior_summary: U8P_NSHOLLOG::look handles the player 'U8P_NSHOLLOG::look' interaction for a hollow log object: it prepares a short description string "hollow log ", asks the item/process to 'bark' that string (causing the description to be shown), and then concludes the interaction.

The routine receives an item pointer (item_ptr) as its parameter, uses that pointer as the target for the display call, and then performs a short implies/pop sequence before suspending/returning — indicating the interaction is complete and control is yielded back to the engine.

There are opcodes in the disassembly that appear to set per-item/process state (set info and process-exclude) before calling Item::bark; this likely configures the object for the display/interaction context but is an inferred behavior.
notable_control_flow: On entry (Look event) construct literal string "hollow log ".
Call Item::bark with the item pointer and the constructed string to show the description.
Capture Item::bark return value, use it in an implies/pop pattern, then suspend/return to end the event handler.

Disassembly shows preceding opcodes that appear to set item/process info and exclude the object from processing; this likely precedes the bark and guards against duplicate processing while the bark runs.
open_questions: Does Item::bark target the player process (viewer) or the item process itself for delivering the message in this context?
Exactly which item/process info field(s) are set by the observed 'set info' opcode(s), and what are the side effects of the 'process exclude' opcode in runtime terms?
What does the implies/pop sequence's result control at the engine level (interrupt propagation, return value semantics, or visual timing)?

main_function.name: U8P_NSHOLLOG::look
main_function.purpose: Respond to the Look verb for a hollow log by producing/displaying the short item description and ending the interaction.
entry_conditions: Triggered by the Look event (Event 0) when an actor/player inspects the hollow log object.

function_map.name: U8P_NSHOLLOG::look
function_map.role: Handle the 'U8P_NSHOLLOG::look' interaction for the hollow log: build and present the object's short description then conclude the event.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine/item routine invoked to display or emit a short single-line message/label for an item/process.

external_calls.purpose: Display the short description string "hollow log " to the appropriate recipient (viewer/process).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_NSHOLLOG::look handles the player 'U8P_NSHOLLOG::look' interaction for a hollow log object: it prepares a short description string "hollow log ", asks the item/process to 'bark' that string (causing the description to be shown), and then concludes the interaction.
